name: Júcar River Gorge Trail from El Picazo
name_original: Ribera del río Júcar desde El Picazo
description: Easy 10.7-mile touring cycle along the Júcar River from El Picazo, offering lush scenery and views of Alarcón Castle in 1 hour 35 minutes.
region_intro: Cycling routes El Picazo Spain are characterized by the dramatic gorges of the Júcar River and the surrounding rolling countryside of Cuenca province. The region features varied terrain, including paths along riverbanks and routes that traverse agricultural lands. Historical landmarks such as Alarcón Castle are often visible from cycling paths, adding cultural interest. The landscape provides a mix of flat sections and moderate ascents suitable for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around El Picazo The most popular touring cycling route is Júcar River Gorge Trail from El Picazo, a 10.7 miles (17.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the scenic Júcar River. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Valera de Abajo from Alarcón — circular by the shore of the Embalse Alarcón, a difficult 48.4 miles (77.9 km) path. This route offers views of the Alarcón Reservoir and the historic Alarcón Castle. Local touring cyclists also love the Bike loop from Casas de Benítez, a 32.1 miles (51.7 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around El Picazo is defined by the Júcar River gorges, historical castles, and rolling agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Picazo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around El Picazo?
A: There are over 14 touring cycling routes around El Picazo, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes explore the dramatic gorges of the Júcar River and the rolling countryside of Cuenca province.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near El Picazo?
A: The terrain around El Picazo is varied, featuring paths along riverbanks, routes that traverse agricultural lands, and sections through the dramatic gorges of the Júcar River. You'll find a mix of flat sections and moderate ascents, suitable for touring cyclists.

Q: What historical landmarks and natural features can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of significant landmarks and natural beauty. You can expect to see impressive sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5611845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henchidero Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de La Losa on the Júcar River&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also pass through the stunning Hoces del Júcar Natural Area.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in El Picazo?
A: Yes, for an easier ride suitable for families, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363965751/jucar-river-gorge-trail-from-el-picazo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Gorge Trail from El Picazo&lt;/a&gt;. This easy 10.7-mile (17.2 km) route follows the scenic Júcar River, offering a pleasant experience for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes around El Picazo?
A: While many outdoor areas in El Picazo are dog-friendly, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near wildlife or other cyclists. Ensure you bring enough water for both yourself and your pet, as specific amenities for dogs might not be available on all routes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40302489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Casas de Benítez&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 32.1-mile (51.7 km) trail leading through the local countryside. Another is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086183277/valera-de-abajo-from-alarcon-circular-by-the-shore-of-the-embalse-alarcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valera de Abajo from Alarcón — circular by the shore of the Embalse Alarcón&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the Alarcón Reservoir.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a cycling tour in El Picazo?
A: El Picazo generally offers parking facilities within the town, especially near central areas or trailheads. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information.

Q: Is public transport available to El Picazo for cyclists with bikes?
A: Public transport options to El Picazo, particularly those accommodating bicycles, can be limited. It's best to check with local bus services in the Cuenca province for their specific policies regarding bicycle transport before planning your trip.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in El Picazo?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant weather for touring cycling in El Picazo, with milder temperatures and less extreme heat than summer. These seasons provide ideal conditions to enjoy the natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its scenic vistas, particularly along the Júcar River gorges. Routes often feature natural viewpoints offering spectacular perspectives of the river, the surrounding landscapes, and landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4663271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that start directly from El Picazo?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363965751/jucar-river-gorge-trail-from-el-picazo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Gorge Trail from El Picazo&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option that begins directly in El Picazo, making it convenient for those staying in the town.

Q: Are permits required for cycling in the Hoces del Júcar Natural Area?
A: While general cycling on established paths typically does not require a permit, it's always wise to check local regulations for specific areas within the Hoces del Júcar Natural Area, especially if you plan to venture off designated routes or into protected zones. Information can usually be found on the regional government's environmental department website.
tips: Beautiful place on the banks of the Júcar River, on the west bank you will find the old La Losa mill that offers a place to take a break and enjoy this beautiful place with plenty of shade and fresh water (there is a fountain).

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: This easy touring bicycle route along the Júcar River is approximately 17.2 kilometers long and generally takes about 1 hour and 35 minutes to complete at a moderate pace.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Ribera del río Júcar desde El Picazo trail is categorized as an easy bike ride, making it suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners and families. The mostly paved surfaces ensure a comfortable and accessible experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ribera del río Júcar desde El Picazo trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, given the trail's starting point near El Picazo, you can typically find parking within the village or at accessible points along the riverbank where the route begins. Look for designated public parking spots.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling along the Júcar River near El Picazo?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly flat with mostly paved surfaces, ideal for touring bicycles. While the Júcar River's upper reaches are mountainous, this section near El Picazo is flatter. You might encounter some 'carreterines' (small roads) in excellent condition, often shaded by trees, and the route passes through lush riparian vegetation and agricultural fields.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, benefiting from the shade provided by riverside trees.

faqs.question: Are there any notable sights or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers serene views of the Júcar River, known for its often striking green waters, as it winds through the landscape. A highlight along the broader Júcar River area is the View of Alarcón Castle and the Júcar River, offering a picturesque vista.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with sections of other official trails. You will find it overlaps with parts of the Sisante trail, the Cañada Real entre olivos y viñedos (covering about 19% of the route), Carretar La Losa (18%), and the Cañada Real de Andalucía (6%).

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Ribera del río Júcar desde El Picazo cycling trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on public trails in this region, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, to ensure their safety and respect for the local environment.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Hoz del Júcar area?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on this trail or in the general Hoz del Júcar area. It's a publicly accessible route for outdoor recreation.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this easy touring bicycle ride, we recommend bringing water, snacks, sun protection (sunscreen, hat), a basic bike repair kit, and a fully charged phone. Comfortable cycling attire and appropriate footwear are also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from El Picazo, a village where you can find local amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and possibly public restrooms. It's advisable to stock up on supplies before starting your ride, as facilities directly along the river path may be limited.

waypoints.name: View of Alarcón Castle and the Júcar River
